name: use-gemini-for-code-analysis
description: 【大型代码库分析专用】当需要分析整个项目架构、识别代码模式、追踪功能实现、梳理模块关系时必须使用本技能。触发关键词：分析整个项目/代码库、架构梳理、模块划分、代码模式识别、功能追踪、全局扫描。本技能通过 Gemini CLI 快速扫描完整代码库，提供 AI 驱动的架构洞察和代码分析，大幅节省手动探索时间。仅返回原始分析结果，不做二次解读。
capabilities: Execute Gemini CLI commands to analyze entire codebases with AI assistance
Identify code patterns, architectural decisions, and module relationships
Trace feature implementations across multiple files and directories
Scan for code quality issues, performance bottlenecks, and security concerns
Generate architecture overviews and component mapping for large projects
Support custom analysis prompts in multiple languages including Chinese
securitySummary: This skill is a CLI wrapper for the Gemini code analysis tool. All static findings were false positives: cryptographic algorithm warnings referred to metadata hashes, high entropy was due to Chinese documentation, and shell commands were documentation examples. The skill executes external commands legitimately through the gemini CLI for code analysis purposes.
valueStatement: Manually exploring large codebases wastes time and effort. This skill integrates Gemini CLI to provide instant AI-driven architecture analysis, pattern detection, and code relationship mapping across entire projects.
userTitle: Analyze Codebases with AI-Powered Gemini CLI Integration
limitations: Requires Gemini CLI to be installed and configured on the system
Analysis quality depends on Gemini CLI capabilities and configuration
Does not interpret or post-process analysis results, returns raw output
Limited to features and file types supported by Gemini CLI

useCases.title: Architecture Review for New Team Members
useCases.description: Help developers joining a project quickly understand the overall architecture, main components, data flow patterns, and key design decisions without spending days reading code.

useCases.title: Security Audit Pattern Detection
useCases.description: Scan large codebases to identify potential security vulnerabilities, code smells, or anti-patterns that need attention before production deployment.

useCases.title: Legacy Code Modernization Planning
useCases.description: Analyze legacy systems to identify outdated patterns, technical debt, and refactoring opportunities to prioritize modernization efforts effectively.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Pattern Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Search this codebase for all uses of the observer pattern
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding specific design patterns across the project

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Overview
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the overall architecture of this application and identify main components, data flow, and directory structure
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting a high-level understanding of project structure

promptTemplates.title: Feature Implementation Trace
promptTemplates.prompt: Trace the complete implementation of the user authentication feature, showing all involved files and integration points
promptTemplates.scenario: Understanding how a specific feature works end-to-end

promptTemplates.title: Security and Performance Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan for potential performance bottlenecks, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues throughout the codebase
promptTemplates.scenario: Comprehensive code quality assessment before release

bestPractices: Specify clear and focused analysis goals in your prompt to get more actionable results
Use specific keywords when searching for patterns or features to improve accuracy
Review the raw output carefully as the skill does not filter or interpret results
antiPatterns: Asking overly broad questions like analyze everything without specific focus areas
Expecting the skill to interpret or explain Gemini output, it only returns raw results
Running analysis without having Gemini CLI properly installed and authenticated

faq.question: What is Gemini CLI and do I need to install it separately?
faq.answer: Gemini CLI is Google's AI-powered code analysis tool. Yes, you must install and configure it independently before using this skill. The skill acts as a wrapper to execute Gemini commands.

faq.question: Does this skill work with any programming language?
faq.answer: The skill supports any language that Gemini CLI can analyze. This typically includes most popular programming languages, but check Gemini CLI documentation for specific language support.

faq.question: Will the skill interpret or summarize the analysis results?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed as a CLI wrapper that returns raw Gemini output without interpretation. You or your primary AI assistant will need to review and process the results.

faq.question: Can I analyze codebases with sensitive or proprietary code?
faq.answer: Analysis happens through Gemini CLI on your local system, but be aware that Gemini may send code to Google servers depending on your configuration. Review Gemini CLI privacy settings before analyzing sensitive code.

faq.question: How large can the codebase be for analysis?
faq.answer: Size limits depend on Gemini CLI capabilities and your system resources. The skill uses the all-files flag which attempts to analyze the entire repository. Very large repos may take longer or hit token limits.

faq.question: Can I customize the analysis prompts or add new analysis types?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide any custom prompt through Claude Code when invoking this skill. The skill will construct the appropriate gemini CLI command with your prompt and execute it.
